mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate output and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC turns negative during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 198.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 198.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth, which sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand growth across production and consumption sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO drops > 1.0%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during expansion phases.
